lesson.menu_title: Who is Daniela? Diagnostic + Personal Introduction
lesson.menu_desc: Introduce yourself with confidence — name, role, company, city and interests in professional English.
lesson.subtitle: Aula 1 — Personal Introduction: Who is Daniela?
lesson.title_tag: Professor View -- Daniela Feitoza | Aula 1 -- Personal Introduction

listenings.text: Hi Daniela, it is Sarah, your English coach. Welcome to your first lesson. Today, listen to one short voice note about introducing yourself in English. A good introduction has four simple parts. First, your name. Say, Hi, I am Daniela. Nice to meet you. Second, your role and company. Say, I am an IT Manager at SPF Group. Third, where you are based. Say, I am based in Sao Paulo, Brazil. And fourth, one personal interest. Say, In my free time, I enjoy going to the beach. Here is a key tip for you. To talk about your job, use the present simple. I manage the team. I am responsible for our systems. To say where your company is, use based in. We are based in Sao Paulo. And remember, your background means your experience and education. My background is in information technology. So, next time you meet someone at a conference, do not say, I do computer things. Say, I am an IT Manager and I am responsible for our retail systems. Short, clear and confident. Try it. Introduce yourself in four parts. Talk soon.

Hello Daniela, this is Marcus from the SAP team. We met at the conference in Orlando last week, and I am leaving you a quick voice note. Let me introduce myself properly. I am Marcus Reed, and I am a Solutions Consultant at a software company based in Chicago. My background is in retail technology, and I am responsible for helping companies improve their store systems. I really enjoyed our conversation about your work. If I remember correctly, you said you are an IT Manager at SPF Group, and your company distributes Centauro and Nike across Brazil. That is a great profile. I manage projects with several retail clients, so I think we have a lot in common. I would love to continue our conversation about new solutions for your retail operations. Please give me a call when you have time. It was a pleasure to meet you, Daniela. Talk soon.
